title: Breathing with the Ocean
author: Sleep Reader
paragraphs: Close your eyes and imagine you are sitting on a quiet shore. The sand beneath you is cool and soft, shaped by the wind into gentle ridges that cradle your body perfectly. The sky above is a deep, restful blue, fading to silver where it meets the water. There is no one else here. There is nowhere you need to be. The only sound is the ocean, and the ocean is breathing, in and out, in and out, slow and steady, the way it has breathed since the beginning of the world.

Begin to notice your own breath. You do not need to change it or control it. Simply notice it the way you might notice a bird landing on a railing nearby. There it is. Air entering through your nose, cool and light. Air leaving through your mouth, warm and soft. It has been doing this all day without your attention, keeping you alive quietly, faithfully, like a candle burning in a window you forgot was lit.

Now let your breath begin to follow the ocean. As the wave rolls in toward the shore, breathe in. Feel the air fill your lungs the way the water fills the shallow pools among the rocks. There is no hurry. The wave does not rush. It simply arrives, spreading itself thin and wide across the sand, reaching as far as it can before it rests. Let your inhale do the same. Let it reach into every corner of your chest, your shoulders, the spaces between your ribs.

And as the wave pulls back, breathe out. Feel everything soften. Your shoulders drop a fraction of an inch. Your jaw unclenches, a tension you did not know you were holding dissolving like salt in warm water. The wave draws back across the sand with a gentle hiss, taking with it whatever it finds, a shell, a pebble, a line drawn in the sand. Let your exhale carry something away too. A worry. A task. A thought that has been circling all day with nowhere to land.

With each breath, you settle deeper into the sand. It is not sinking. It is more like the sand is rising gently to meet you, supporting your back, your arms, the backs of your legs. You are being held. The earth beneath you is solid and ancient and patient, and it does not mind holding you for as long as you need. You are not heavy. You are exactly the right weight for this moment.

The ocean continues its rhythm. Wave in, wave out. You do not need to count. You do not need to measure. The ocean has never lost its rhythm in all the years it has been moving, and it will not lose it now. You can trust it completely. Let it set the pace. Let your breath be a guest in the ocean's house, following its customs, moving when it moves, resting when it rests.

As you breathe, imagine the tension in your body as a color. Perhaps it is grey, or red, or a dull bronze. With each exhale, that color flows out of you and into the sand, where it is absorbed and dissolved, returned to the earth. With each inhale, a new color enters, something soft and cool, the pale blue of early morning, the silver-white of moonlight. It spreads through your body like watercolor on wet paper, reaching your fingers, your toes, the top of your head.

Your thoughts may still come. That is all right. Thoughts are like clouds passing over the ocean. They do not change the water. They do not stop the waves. Let them pass. You do not need to follow them or push them away. Simply notice them, the way you notice a gull gliding overhead, and then return your attention to the breath. In and out. In and out. The wave and the breath, the breath and the wave.

The light is changing now. The silver sky has deepened to a soft violet, and the first stars are beginning to appear, faint and far away, like memories of light. The ocean darkens to a deep, peaceful indigo, and the sound of the waves becomes even softer, as though the water too is preparing for sleep. Your breath has slowed without your trying. It moves now like a tide in a small, sheltered bay, barely a ripple, barely a sound.

You are warm. You are safe. You are held by the sand and the shore and the steady rhythm of a world that has been turning long before you arrived and will continue long after, and that in this moment asks nothing of you except that you rest. The ocean breathes. You breathe. There is no difference between the two. There is only this, the wave, the breath, the quiet, the dark. Goodnight. Let the ocean carry you gently into sleep. Sweet dreams.
